stages.name: [KEEP] Re-verify generic nomenclature REST seam (/api/nomenclature/<key>/) for the 9 L1 catalog keys
stages.acceptance: VERIFY-ONLY KEEP stage — prove the EXISTING behavior in reality on the new deps; ship NO production code fix here. Prove IN REALITY (running test ERP, authenticated — an unregistered device 403s before routing, so anonymous curl cannot judge mount) that the existing generic nomenclature CRUD seam at `/api/nomenclature/<key>/` already serves the nine Layer-1 catalog keys with the exact frozen contract every L1 frontend stage will build against, then FREEZE that contract for the layer. The nine real registry keys (verified against `backend/apps/nomenclature/registry.py` + models): `currency`, `bank`, `direction`, `expense_category`, `payment_category`, `cash_desk_type`, `bank_account_type`, `price_level`, `contract_type` (the last is registered in `apps/parties/models.py` but served by the same generic endpoint). Confirm the shape: GET `/api/nomenclature/<key>/` lists, POST creates, GET `/api/nomenclature/<key>/<pk>/` retrieves, PUT `/api/nomenclature/<key>/<pk>/` does a FULL-OBJECT update — and that SOFT-DELETE is `PUT active=false` while DELETE and PATCH both answer 405 by design (`NomenclatureDetailView.http_method_names = [get,put,head,options]`; `test_api.py` already asserts the collection-DELETE 405 — extend TEST coverage to all nine keys + a per-key PATCH/DELETE-405 + a PUT active=false round-trip). Confirm the per-key field shape each catalog screen needs surfaces correctly through the serializer (`fields=__all__`): `payment_category.code` + `direction` IN/OUT enum; `expense_category.code` + `direction` FK→Direction; `cash_desk_type.code` + `requires_pj` bool; `direction.code`; `currency.code`; `bank.code`; `bank_account_type.code`; `price_level.algorithm_key`; `contract_type` fields — and that model `full_clean()` validation errors (e.g. duplicate `code`) come back through the API error envelope, since there is NO separate field-metadata/OPTIONS endpoint (the FE framework drives config-per-entity, not server introspection). This stage is routine = VERIFY-ONLY and gets NO post-build code audit, so a code change must not ship here: if a key is missing/mismounted or PATCH/DELETE do NOT 405 — i.e. a defect requiring a CODE change is found — ESCALATE it for a separate structural fix stage rather than patching in place. The frozen seam (endpoint shape + 405 policy + soft-delete-via-PUT + the per-key writable field set) is the CONTRACT root the whole layer depends on; record it precisely in the .md.
acceptance_criteria: All nine keys (currency, bank, direction, expense_category, payment_category, cash_desk_type, bank_account_type, price_level, contract_type) confirmed reachable+CRUD-capable via /api/nomenclature/<key>/ on the running test ERP, authenticated.
GET(list)/POST/GET(detail)/PUT(full-object) verified per key; PATCH and DELETE both return 405 for every key (test coverage extended beyond the single existing currency assertion).
Soft-delete proven as PUT active=false (row deactivates, stays referenceable); no DELETE/PATCH affordance is or will be used anywhere.

Per-key writable field shape surfaces correctly (payment_category.direction IN/OUT, expense_category.direction FK, cash_desk_type.requires_pj, price_level.algorithm_key, *.code) and clean() errors return through the standard API error envelope.
No field-metadata/OPTIONS endpoint is assumed — the contract explicitly delegates field/column definitions to FE config-per-entity (documented for the framework stage).

VERIFY-ONLY: prove the existing behavior in reality on the new deps; if a defect requiring a CODE change is found (missing/mismounted key, wrong 405), ESCALATE for a separate structural fix stage — never an in-place fix here.
The frozen contract (endpoint shape + 405 policy + soft-delete-via-PUT + nine-key field map) is documented in the companion .md as the layer's seam.

stages.name: [NEW] Generic config-driven CRUD catalog framework SKELETON on ONE entity (Currency) — the reusable visual tone-setter
stages.acceptance: Build the ONE reusable, config-driven catalog screen that every Layer-1 (and later L2/L4) nomenclature instantiates, and prove it end-to-end on a SINGLE entity — Currency — against the frozen `/api/nomenclature/<key>/` seam. This is the visual TONE-SETTER for the whole app: it runs the FULL UI/UX gate against the founder reference apps (NetSuite/Dynamics/Odoo — density + role-model, executed clean), and its config-per-entity descriptor type is the second frozen contract of the layer (the FE seam the fan-out stage drives with config only, no new framework code). Deliverables: (1) a generic `nomenclature.ts` API client (list/create/update via apiFetch over the seam, full-object PUT, soft-delete = PUT active=false; never PATCH/DELETE) returning the paginated `{count,next,previous,results}` envelope; (2) a `CatalogScreen` component driven by an `EntityConfig` descriptor — `{ key, titleRo, columns: ColumnDef[], formFields: FieldDef[] }` where FieldDef covers text/number/select/switch/entity-picker input kinds chosen for MINIMUM clicks (binary → switch not dropdown, ui-ux-laws 9-g) — rendering a LIST (AppTable, dense, exception-first, sortable, active/inactive state visible) + a create/edit DRAWER (AppDrawer + AppForm, full-object PUT on save, optimistic update + instant feedback, validation surfaced from the API error envelope at the right moment, draft never lost) + an explicit DEACTIVATE action (PUT active=false, with reactivate) + an 'adaugă nou' entry point; (3) wire it for Currency as the proving instance (the `currency` config object). The EntityConfig + FieldDef + ColumnDef descriptor types are the CONTRACT — minimal, additive, documented as the public seam; the fan-out stage adds ONLY config objects against them. All styling on tokens + approved components (no raw antd, no hardcoded colors/spacing — mechanical guards green). Standing law baked in: list/drawer expose edit + deactivate(soft-delete via PUT active=false) + the change is auditable/reversible; every entity-picker FieldDef supports add-new-inline (proven generically so instantiations inherit it). Full UI/UX gate: §2 questionnaire (operator role/device, the 500th-use core loop, keyboard operability), all states (loading/error/empty/disabled), desktop+phone capture self-loop. Since this is an 'important screen', it goes to the founder visual review.
acceptance_criteria: Generic nomenclature.ts client (list/create/update over /api/nomenclature/<key>/, full-object PUT, soft-delete=PUT active=false, paginated envelope) added to the api layer; never emits PATCH/DELETE.

Reusable CatalogScreen driven by an EntityConfig descriptor (key, titleRo, columns, formFields with text/number/select/switch/entity-picker kinds chosen for minimum clicks) renders list + create/edit drawer + deactivate/reactivate + add-new.
EntityConfig/FieldDef/ColumnDef types are frozen, minimal, and documented as the layer's FE contract; the fan-out stage will add config objects only (no framework code).

Currency instantiated as the single proving entity: create, edit (full-object PUT), deactivate (PUT active=false) and reactivate all work against the live seam with optimistic update + instant feedback.

Standing law satisfied generically: edit + soft-delete(PUT active=false, never PATCH/DELETE→405) + reversibility present, and entity-picker fields support add-new-inline so instantiations inherit both.

Only tokens + approved components used (no raw antd, no hardcoded styles); mechanical UI/UX guards pass; all states loading/error/empty/disabled handled; keyboard-operable, dense per operator philosophy.
Full UI/UX questionnaire answered + desktop+phone self-capture clean + vitest covers the framework (list/drawer/deactivate/add-new/validation) on Currency; flagged for founder visual review.

stages.name: [NEW] Instantiate the remaining 8 L1 catalogs on the framework — config-per-entity only, no new framework code
stages.acceptance: Drive the `crud-framework-skeleton` CatalogScreen for the other eight Layer-1 nomenclature keys by adding ONLY config objects against the frozen EntityConfig/FieldDef/ColumnDef seam — no new framework code, no new visual primitives. The eight keys + their distinguishing config: `bank` (code/name); `direction` (code/name — the body/service/general tag table, labelled distinctly from PaymentCategory's IN/OUT so the founder never conflates the two 'direction' concepts, per DRAFT Q8); `expense_category` (code + a `direction` entity-picker FieldDef→Direction catalog, with add-new-inline back into the direction catalog); `payment_category` (code + a `direction` IN/OUT switch/segmented control — binary, minimum clicks, NOT a dropdown); `cash_desk_type` (code + a `requires_pj` switch — the flag CashDesk.clean() keys on); `bank_account_type` (code/name); `price_level` (name + `algorithm_key`); `contract_type` (its fields). Register each as a CatalogScreen instance in the L0 menu registry under the `nomenclatoare` group with correct order + Romanian labels (catalog/entity-centric navigation per ui-ux-laws §7.4). Each instantiation inherits — at zero extra cost — list + create/edit drawer + deactivate (PUT active=false) + add-new-inline from the framework; the stage's job is correct per-entity column/field config + labels + registry wiring, plus a thin smoke test per entity. All styling inherited from the framework (tokens + approved components only; mechanical guards green). Standing law is satisfied by inheritance (edit/soft-delete present on every catalog; pickers add-new-inline) — assert it holds per entity rather than re-implementing. Full UI/UX gate applies but is largely satisfied by the shared framework; the questionnaire focuses on per-entity field/control fit (e.g. IN/OUT as a switch, requires_pj as a switch) and the distinct labelling of the two direction concepts; desktop+phone capture spot-check. This is the stage the founder gate exercises: create/edit/soft-delete currency, payment categories (one IN + one OUT), expense category, a cash-desk type with requires_pj=true — persisted + appearing in pickers.
acceptance_criteria: Eight config objects (bank, direction, expense_category, payment_category, cash_desk_type, bank_account_type, price_level, contract_type) added against the existing EntityConfig seam — no new framework/component code, no new visual primitives.

Per-entity control fit honoured: payment_category.direction = IN/OUT switch (binary, min clicks), cash_desk_type.requires_pj = switch, expense_category.direction = entity-picker→Direction with add-new-inline; price_level uses algorithm_key.
The two 'direction' concepts (PaymentCategory IN/OUT enum vs the Direction tag table) are labelled distinctly in the UI so they cannot be conflated.
Each catalog registered in the L0 menu registry under the nomenclatoare group with correct order + Romanian labels (entity-centric).
Every catalog inherits list + create/edit (full-object PUT) + deactivate (PUT active=false, never PATCH/DELETE) + add-new-inline pickers from the framework; verified per entity, not re-implemented.

Founder-gate path works end-to-end: create/edit/soft-delete a currency, two payment categories (IN+OUT), an expense category, and a cash-desk type with requires_pj=true — all persist and reappear in pickers.

Thin smoke test per entity (config renders + one create/edit/deactivate round-trip) green; tokens/approved-components only; per-entity UI/UX control-fit questionnaire answered; desktop+phone spot-check clean.
